title: Tylanthus atratus
intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Tylanthus atratus. However, I can provide information on a plant with a similar name - Tylophora atrata, or Tylophora, also known as Tylanthus.

Tylophora is a plant that belongs to the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. It is a climbing shrub native to tropical regions of Asia. The health benefits of Tylophora include its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antihelmintic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The bioactive constituents of Tylophora include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Tylophora include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory disorders.
